content.full_text:
JOHN SETTER

550 Orange Avenue, #C
Coronado, CA 92118
(619) 435-1437

March 13, 1992

Fair Political Practices Committee
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Sir/Madam:

Enclosed is an outline of criteria which an established committee
has been directed to achieve by City Council.

The three questions at the end pertain to the outline.

A timely response will be greatly appreciated, since we are
anxious to start the process without controversy.

Thank you,

John Setter

Enclosure

MAR 17 11:09 AM '92

CITYGATE COMMITTEE

Purpose: Organize program draft of "Request for Proposals".
When approved by City Council, will be sent out to the
public.

Outline a Draft Request for Design Proposals for a
public project.

All entries anonymously entered are to be evaluated by
public and City Council. (Council to establish start
dates). Selected proposal will not receive a contract
or compensation.

- Organize all related background and design criteria, such as:

- Established City design criteria (Title 17,
General Plan, Downtown Guidelines, etc.)

- Request all Cal Trans design criteria as to the
specifics of the site and project use.

- Request all City Planning and Engineering Department design criteria as to the specifics of the
site and project use.

- Prior to and during public forums, notice and
request all or any design criteria input from:

- City Design Review
- Community Groups
- General Public
- AIA, etc.

- Gather concerns on estimates of time which might
be allowed for each stage of the program, such as:

- Public notice deadlines

- Design development time allowances for
first stage, second stage, etc.

- Public forum time frames for public
viewing, discussions, voting, etc.

1

-    Format presentation standards, drafting standards,
     for finalist.

-    Outline order of events with time frame prefer-
     ence.

-    Request outline of City of Coronado responsibili-
     ties/Applicant's responsibilities from City.

                              QUESTIONS

1.   If a person participates on the committee, can such member
     submit a proposal?

2.   Can persons who are members of the attending public meeting
     and who participate in the process, submit a proposal?

3.   If the proposals received by the City are submitted to the
     Design Review Commission for evaluation, can a Design Review
     Commissioner submit a proposal?




                                    2

sections.question:
1. If a person participates on the committee, can such member submit a proposal?

2. Can persons who are members of the attending public meeting and who participate in the process, submit a proposal?

3. If the proposals received by the City are submitted to the Design Review Commission for evaluation, can a Design Review Commissioner submit a proposal?
